places.name: Kobe Harborland

places.seo_phrase: Kobe Harborland — Waterfront Shopping and Night Views

places.name: Mount Rokko
places.description: A mountain ridge behind Kobe famous for night views, alpine plants, and cool summer retreats.
places.seo_phrase: Mount Rokko — Panoramic Views Over Kobe and Osaka Bay

places.name: Nunobiki Falls
places.description: A dramatic cascade of four waterfalls in the hills behind Shin-Kobe Station.
places.seo_phrase: Nunobiki Falls — Sacred Waterfalls Above Kobe

places.name: Kobe Port Tower
places.description: A 108-meter red steel tower shaped like a Japanese drum, symbol of Kobe's port.
places.seo_phrase: Kobe Port Tower — Iconic Red Lattice Harbor Landmark

places.name: Nankinmachi

places.seo_phrase: Nankinmachi — Kobe's Vibrant Chinatown

places.name: Arima Onsen
places.description: A historic mountain onsen town with famous golden and silver thermal waters.
places.seo_phrase: Arima Onsen — Japan's Oldest Hot Spring Town

places.name: Meriken Park
places.description: A waterfront park with modern art, the Port Tower, and memorials to the 1995 Great Hanshin Earthquake.
places.seo_phrase: Meriken Park — Harbor Promenade and Earthquake Memorial

places.name: Kitano-cho
places.description: A hillside district of ornate Western-style mansions opened when Kobe became an international port.
places.seo_phrase: Kitano-cho — Western Residences and Ijinkan Houses
places.commons_file: Weathercock House Kobe Kitano Ijinkan 風見鶏の館（旧トーマス住宅）.jpg
places.wiki_story: Kitano-chō or Kitano Ijinkan is a historical district in Kobe, Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan, which contains a number of foreign residences from the late Meiji and early Taishō eras of Japanese history. While the term ijinkan can refer to any foreign residence of this period in Japan, it usually refers to those of Kitano given the number and high concentration of those that remain.

places.name: Kobe Earthquake Memorial Museum
places.wiki_title: The Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake Memorial Disaster Reduction and Human Renovation Institution
places.description: A moving museum documenting the 1995 Great Hanshin Earthquake and Kobe's recovery.
places.seo_phrase: Kobe Earthquake Memorial — Lessons of the 1995 Hanshin Disaster
places.commons_file: Disaster Reduction and Human Renovation Institution.jpg
places.maps_query: Disaster Reduction and Human Renovation Institution, Kobe, Japan
places.wiki_story: The Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake Memorial Disaster Reduction and Human Renovation Institution is the earthquake disaster memorial museum located in Chūō-ku, Kobe (HAT Kobe), Hyōgo Prefecture in Japan. It collects and preserves materials related to the Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake and subsequent reconstruction.
